title: Airstrikes on Iran: How the US and Israel hope to bring down the regime
subhead: The goal of the US-Israeli strikes is ambitious. The top target on their list was Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, whose death was confirmed by Iranian state media on Sunday. Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu have also targeted the country's ballistic missile capabilities.

title: Gulf monarchies face a dilemma after Iran attacks them in retaliation for US-Israeli strikes
subhead: The Arabian Peninsula's monarchs now face a painful dilemma: Whether to continue trying to appease Tehran, or more closely align themselves with the US and Israel, though this could expose them to further strikes from their neighbor.

title: French domestic security forces placed on high alert following airstrikes on Iran
subhead: French Interior Minister Laurent Nuñez instructed prefects to 'report to intelligence services any influence or destabilization efforts linked to this conflict' following the strikes in the Middle East.

title: Bombings in Iran raise fears of oil crisis
subhead: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz and US-Israeli strikes in Iran are reigniting the specter of a surge in crude oil prices. But the global economy is less vulnerable today than it was during the 1973 oil crisis.

title: Attacks on Iran: US and Israel are committing 'a crime of aggression,' says Bernie Sanders' former adviser
subhead: An influential voice in the progressive camp and former advisor to Senator Bernie Sanders, Matthew Duss harshly criticizes Donald Trump's actions following Saturday's strikes, stating that 'this war is unfortunately the culmination of decades of a militarist foreign policy.'

Ali Khamenei, Iran's supreme leader and symbol of a repressive regime, has died
subhead: The successor to Ayatollah Khomeini in 1989, Ali Khamenei embodied the Islamic Republic, setting its political course both domestically and abroad. Over the course of his rule, and faced with the country's isolation, he chose to harden the regime and brutally repress any dissent. He died on Saturday, February 28, at the age of 86.

title: EU-Mercosur trade deal set to take effect without waiting for European Parliament vote
subhead: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen fast-tracked the announcement on Friday, much to France's dismay.

title: David W. Bates, cybernetics specialist: 'The brain is not a computer'
subhead: With the advent of artificial intelligence, the American academic aims to avoid the pitfalls of both technophobia and transhumanism. In his view, it is necessary to move beyond a simplistic opposition between 'human' or 'natural' intelligence and AI.

title: Trump's war of choice in Iran
'The Louvre director's resignation raises an old question: Why do cultural execs go mad?'
Sahar Delijani, writer: 'The Iranian people's struggle against tyranny has become a test of our politics'
